content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>幫我補回來</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-03</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
驗證完成,我把這兩天的改動查清楚了。以下是結論。

## 結論

型別檢查 ✅ 通過,44 個 smoke test 裡 43 個實質正常。但發現 **1 個真實且隱形的 merge 回退**,加上 1 個資料漂移、1 個環境限制。

---

## 🔴 主要問題:upstream merge 靜默回退了本地「tool observation seq 層」

**證據鏈(A 級,literal + git 三方比對):**

| ref | `tool-observer.ts` 有 recordSeqObservation | `sessionManager.ts` 呼叫 |
|-----|:-:|:-:|
| 本地 pre-merge tip `f7f2a09` | ✅ 1 | ✅ 3 |
| upstream tip `4118f1d` | ❌ 0 | ❌ 0 |
| **merge 結果 `d22a568`(HEAD)** | **❌ 0** | **❌ 0** |

merge 時 `tool-observer.ts` 與 `sessionManager.ts` 未進衝突判定,git 直接採用了 **upstream 版**,把你本地 `a855fee`(feat: tool-usage observer Phase 1 dual-tier)做的 **Tier A seq 層**連同呼叫端一起蓋掉。

**為什麼隱形:** 函式和它的 2 處呼叫端「一起」消失,型別一致 → `tsc` 過、build 過。唯一警報是孤兒測試 `check-tool-observer.mjs`(重建 dist + 帶 env 後仍 `recordSeqObservation is not a function`)。

**實際影響(界定過):**
- 當年設計是雙軌:**seq 層專供 ACP 路徑**(Kiro / Codex / Claude Agent 三個 adapter 都走 ACP),rich 層(`recordRichObservation`,只在 `provider/tool-loop.ts`)供 Direct 路徑。
- 現在 ACP 路徑的 `tool_call` handler(`sessionManager.ts:424-451`)**完全不寫 observation**,連原本的 `logEvent("tool_call")` 也一併沒了。
- ∴ **在 ACP adapter 下(即三個主要 adapter),`observations.jsonl` 不再被寫入**。消費端 `dream.ts`、`event-log.ts` 的 `getToolUsageStats`(`/utilityreport` 工具統計、dream 報告)在 ACP session 會拿到空/殘缺資料。只有較次要的 Direct provider 仍有記錄。

這需要你決定:是**有意**用 upstream 的 observer 設計取代雙軌(那就該刪掉孤兒測試),還是 merge **誤蓋**(那就該還原 seq 層 + 兩處呼叫端)。從「留著會失敗的測試 + ACP 是主力路徑」判斷,較像誤蓋,但我不臆測——見下方詢問。

---

## 🟡 次要 / 環境(非程式碼缺陷)

- **`check-topic-match`**:帶 `--env-file` 後報 `topic shard count != classify count — run rebuild-topic-shards.mjs`。這是 `G:\AI\AIMemory` 裡 topic 分片的**資料漂移**,跑一次 `rebuild-topic-shards.mjs` 即可,與這兩天程式碼無關。
- **`check-acp-model-effort`**:本機安裝的 Kiro CLI 回 `-32601 Method not found (session/set_config_option)`。model/effort pin 功能(upstream `57b3daa`/`c524d51`)對這個 CLI 版本是**優雅 no-op**(程式碼有 `not applied (ignored)` 處理,不 crash)。要真生效需較新的 CLI。程式碼本身沒問題。
- **假警報(我先前沒帶 `--env-file` 造成的)**:`check-acp-trace-orphan` 帶 env 後 ✅ 通過。這些測試預設 fallback 到不存在的 `F:\AI\AIMemory`(本機是 `G:`),屬跑法問題不是 bug。

---

## ✅ 正常的部分

- `tsc --noEmit` 乾淨、`tsc -p .` build 乾淨。
- merge 其餘衝突解決(`acpClient` pid getter、`misc.ts` reset clean、`config.ts` bridge config 路徑集中化、`dream.ts` 結構化回傳 + `/docupdate`、`event-log` stats)都是雙方各加不同東西、正確併存。
- 43 個 smoke test 實質通過。

---

**建議**:針對主要問題,我傾向**還原 seq 層**(把 `recordSeqObservation` + `SeqObservationInput` 補回 `tool-observer.ts`,並在現行 `sessionManager.ts` 的 ACP `tool_call_update` handler 重接兩處呼叫,對齊 upstream 的新結構)。要我動手嗎?還是你其實已決定改用 upstream 的單軌 observer(那我就改成刪掉孤兒測試)?

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

<summary>
  <request>Restore missing functionality after upstream merge - investigation revealed silent tool observation layer regression</request>
  <investigated>Conducted comprehensive verification of merge result between local tip (f7f2a09) and upstream (4118f1d). Examined tool-observer.ts, sessionManager.ts, and 44 smoke tests. Performed git three-way comparison to identify what was silently reverted during merge to HEAD (d22a568).</investigated>
  <learned>Git merge silently adopted upstream versions of tool-observer.ts and sessionManager.ts without conflict markers, reverting the dual-tier observation system (seq layer for ACP paths, rich layer for Direct paths) that was implemented locally in commit a855fee. The seq layer function recordSeqObservation and its 3 call sites disappeared together, making the regression invisible to TypeScript - types remained consistent, builds passed. Only orphaned test check-tool-observer.mjs flagged the missing function. Impact: ACP adapter paths (Kiro/Codex/Claude Agent) no longer write observations.jsonl, affecting tool usage statistics in /utilityreport and dream reports. Direct provider still records via rich layer.</learned>
  <completed>Verification completed with clear evidence chain. Identified 1 confirmed merge regression (tool observation seq layer), 1 data drift issue (topic shards), 1 environment limitation (Kiro CLI version). Confirmed 43 of 44 tests substantively pass, type checking clean, other merge conflicts (acpClient pid getter, misc.ts reset, config.ts bridge paths, dream.ts structured return, event-log stats) properly resolved.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user decision: restore seq layer (add recordSeqObservation back to tool-observer.ts and reconnect 2 call sites in sessionManager.ts ACP tool_call_update handler) OR adopt upstream's single-track observer design (delete orphaned test). Recommendation leans toward restoration since ACP is the primary adapter path.</next_steps>
  <notes>The regression was architecturally significant but operationally invisible - function and consumers vanished atomically, preventing type errors. The dual-tier design was intentional: seq layer for structured ACP observation, rich layer for Direct provider. Losing seq layer means blind spot in primary usage paths.</notes>
</summary>

    <fact>Current tool-observer.ts only contains rich tier with source: "rich" (not union type)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Extracted the seq observation layer implementation from historical commit a855fee to prepare for restoration. The original dual-tier design had a SeqObservationInput interface for lightweight ACP adapter observations (name, status, sessionId, chatId, precedingTools) and a recordSeqObservation function that wrote observations with source: "seq" and completion-order semantics. Current tool-observer.ts file comparison confirms it only contains the rich tier (RichObservationInput and recordRichObservation) with source hardcoded as "rich" instead of a union type. This extraction provides the source material needed to restore the seq layer that was silently reverted during the upstream merge.</narrative>

  </concepts>

    <file>src/tool-observer.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

It's trying to import `recordSeqObservation` which doesn't exist in the current version. This confirms it's an orphaned test - the code it tests was removed in the merge.

content.thinking:
This is very interesting evidence showing the schema differences across the three git refs:

1. **f7f2a09 (local pre-merge)**: Has the dual-tier system
   - `source: "seq" | "rich"` (union type)
   - `precedingToolsSemantics: PrecedingSemantics` 
   - `PrecedingSemantics = "exec-order" | "completion-order-approx"`
   - `TURN_MARKER` constant exported
   - `status?: "completed" | "failed"` (optional in Observation)
   - `status: "completed" | "failed"` (required in SeqObservationInput)

2. **4118f1d (upstream)** and **d22a568 (merge result/HEAD)**: Both identical
   - `source: "rich"` (literal, not union)
   - No `precedingToolsSemantics`
   - No `TURN_MARKER`
   - No `status` field
   - No `PrecedingSemantics` type

This confirms the regression - the merge adopted upstream's simpler single-tier design and lost all the dual-tier infrastructure.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Schema comparison confirms dual-tier system completely removed in merge</title>
  <subtitle>Local pre-merge had union source type, semantics field, TURN_MARKER, and status; upstream and merge result have none</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Local pre-merge f7f2a09 had source type as union "seq" | "rich" with PrecedingSemantics type and precedingToolsSemantics field</fact>
    <fact>Local pre-merge exported TURN_MARKER constant (" TURN ") for cross-turn separation</fact>
    <fact>Local pre-merge had status field ("completed" | "failed") in observations</fact>
    <fact>Upstream 4118f1d and merge result d22a568 both have source as literal "rich" only</fact>
    <fact>Upstream and merge result have no precedingToolsSemantics, no TURN_MARKER, no status fields</fact>
    <fact>Grep output shows 14 lines of seq/rich infrastructure in f7f2a09 versus 2 lines in 4118f1d and d22a568</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Three-way schema comparison reveals the complete removal of dual-tier observation infrastructure during merge. The local pre-merge commit f7f2a09 had a sophisticated design with union source types discriminating between seq (completion-order-approx for ACP paths) and rich (exec-order for Direct paths), a TURN_MARKER constant to prevent cross-turn tool contamination, status tracking for completed/failed tools, and PrecedingSemantics types to distinguish ordering semantics. Both upstream tip 4118f1d and the merge result d22a568 are identical, containing only the rich tier with source hardcoded to "rich" literal and none of the dual-tier machinery. The merge result exactly matches upstream, confirming git adopted upstream's version wholesale and discarded all local seq tier enhancements.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/tool-observer.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

3. Line 251-260: obsInput is created and recordRichObservation is called

content.thinking:
This is a detailed extraction of the seq observation wiring from the local pre-merge commit. Let me document what was found:

1. _recentToolNames field definition (lines 130-140): A rolling array of tool names plus turn boundary markers, used for Tier A seq observation's precedingTools. Maintained only in tool_call case, resetTurn pushes TURN_MARKER but doesn't clear it.

2. tool_call case (lines 368-380): The title (tool name) is pushed to _recentToolNames using appendToolName. This is the "single maintenance point" to avoid double-counting from tool_call+update.

3. resetTurn (lines 442-455): Clears various per-turn fields but explicitly does NOT clear _recentToolNames. Instead pushes TURN_MARKER to separate turns. Comment explains the rationale: hard clearing would make every turn's first tool have empty precedingTools; the marker lets Phase 2 analyzer detect turn boundaries.

This is the exact source code needed to restore the seq tier in sessionManager.

    <fact>resetTurn pushes TURN_MARKER when array has content, letting Phase 2 analyzer detect turn boundaries</fact>
    <fact>Comment at line 449 warns "致命#3：push turn 邊界 marker 但「不清空」" explaining hard clear would make first tool per turn have empty precedingTools</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Retrieved the complete seq observation wiring implementation from local pre-merge sessionManager. The _recentToolNames field was carefully designed as a rolling sequence that persists across turns, with TURN_MARKER insertions to delimit turn boundaries without losing historical context. The field is maintained in exactly one place (tool_call case) to avoid double-counting tools, since both tool_call and tool_call_update events fire for each tool. The resetTurn logic was sophisticated: it clears per-turn buffers but deliberately preserves _recentToolNames, only inserting a TURN_MARKER separator. This design lets each tool's precedingTools reflect realistic temporal context (recent tools from current and prior turns) while giving the Phase 2 analyzer clear signals to cut tool combinations at turn boundaries. The extracted code provides the exact restoration template for re-wiring seq observations into current sessionManager.</narrative>

2. Lines 417-423: recordSeqObservation for failed status, only when activeProvider === "acp"
3. Both use slice(0, -1) to exclude the current tool from precedingTools (R-2 regression fix)

This is the critical missing code that needs to be restored. The current HEAD sessionManager has no recordSeqObservation calls at all in its tool_call_update handler.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Extracted complete tool_call_update seq observation recording logic</title>
  <subtitle>Pre-merge version recorded seq observations for completed and failed tools in ACP path with precedingTools slice fix</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Lines 399-405 recorded seq observation for completed tools with status: "completed"</fact>
    <fact>Lines 417-423 recorded seq observation for failed tools with status: "failed"</fact>
    <fact>Both branches guarded with "if (session.activeProvider === 'acp')" to avoid double-recording with Direct path's rich tier</fact>
    <fact>Both used slice(0, -1) on _recentToolNames to exclude current tool from precedingTools</fact>
    <fact>R-2 regression fix documented: slice excludes current tool to align with rich tier semantics where preceding does not include self</fact>
    <fact>Observations included sessionId and chatId from session object</fact>
    <fact>Comment at line 398 explains Tier A for ACP, Tier B rich handles Direct in tool-loop (BC-1 behavior contract)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Extracted the complete seq observation recording implementation from pre-merge tool_call_update handler. The code showed sophisticated dual-tier coordination: seq observations only fire when activeProvider is "acp" (avoiding duplication since Direct provider writes rich observations in tool-loop), and precedingTools uses slice(0, -1) to exclude the current tool that was already pushed in the tool_call case. This R-2 regression fix aligns seq semantics with rich tier where precedingTools truly means "preceding" not "including current". Both completed and failed tool paths record observations with appropriate status, using session.sessionId and session.chatId for correlation. The guard comment references BC-1 behavior contract: Direct path should only write rich observations, not seq. This implementation is completely absent from current HEAD sessionManager, leaving ACP adapter paths with zero observation recording despite being the primary adapter type used by all three adapters (Kiro, Codex, Claude Agent).</narrative>

This shows the current state of sessionManager.ts. Key findings:

1. Current has `_consecutiveToolFails` field but NOT `_toolTitles` or `_recentToolNames`
2. Current imports do NOT include anything from tool-observer.ts
3. Current tool_call_update directly uses `toolCallId` as the display name without resolving via _toolTitles
4. Current ChatSession fields show _orphanBuffer, _budgetWarned etc. but no _recentToolNames or _toolTitles

This confirms:
- _toolTitles map is missing (used to resolve toolCallId to human-readable name)
- _recentToolNames is missing (used for precedingTools tracking)
- No imports from tool-observer means no recordSeqObservation available

The current code at lines 435/440 just uses toolCallId directly as the tool name, which is opaque. The a855fee version maintained _toolTitles to map toolCallId → human-readable name.

This is useful evidence showing the infrastructure needed for restoration.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Current sessionManager missing both _toolTitles and _recentToolNames infrastructure</title>
  <subtitle>HEAD directly uses opaque toolCallId as tool name without resolution, no tool-observer imports</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Current ChatSession has _consecutiveToolFails field but lacks _toolTitles Map and _recentToolNames array</fact>
    <fact>Current imports (lines 1-20) include logEvent from event-log.js but nothing from tool-observer.ts</fact>
    <fact>Current tool_call_update at lines 435/440 directly uses toolCallId as display name without resolution</fact>
    <fact>Pre-merge used _toolTitles Map to resolve toolCallId to human-readable name captured at tool_call start</fact>
    <fact>Current ChatSession fields include _orphanBuffer, _budgetWarned but no observation-related fields</fact>
    <fact>Pre-merge imported recordSeqObservation, appendToolName, TURN_MARKER from tool-observer.js</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Confirmed current sessionManager is missing both critical pieces of seq observation infrastructure. The _toolTitles Map, which resolved opaque toolCallIds to human-readable tool names for logging and observation, is completely absent. Current code just displays the raw toolCallId string (lines 435/440), losing readability. More critically, _recentToolNames array for tracking precedingTools across turns is missing, along with all tool-observer imports. Without these fields and imports, there's no foundation to restore seq observation recording. The restoration requires: (1) add tool-observer import line, (2) add _toolTitles and _recentToolNames fields to ChatSession interface, (3) restore _toolTitles.set() in tool_call case, (4) restore appendToolName call in tool_call case, (5) restore recordSeqObservation calls in completed/failed branches of tool_call_update, (6) restore TURN_MARKER logic in resetTurn. This is substantial infrastructure, not a simple one-line fix.</narrative>

Iteration limit (default 25)\\n * 2. Repetition detection (same tool+args ≥3 times → break)\\n * 3. Parallel tool execution (Promise.all for multi-tool responses)\\n */\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { resolve, dirname } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport type { McpPool } from \\\"./mcp-pool.js\\\";\\nimport { enrichCodegraphResult } from \\\"./codegraph-enricher.js\\\";\\nimport { recordRichObservation, appendToolName } from \\\"../tool-observer.js\\\";\\nimport { runPostToolHooks } from \\\"../tool-hooks.js\\\";\\nimport { logEvent } from \\\"../event-log.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types shared with adapters ────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ChatMessage {\\n  role: \\\"system\\\" | \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\" | \\\"tool\\\";\\n  content: string | ContentBlock[];\\n  tool_call_id?: string;\\n  tool_calls?: ToolCall[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ContentBlock {\\n  type: \\\"text\\\" | \\\"tool_use\\\" | \\\"tool_result\\\";\\n  text?: string;\\n  id?: string;\\n  name?: string;\\n  input?: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  tool_use_id?: string;\\n  content?: string;\\n  is_error?: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolCall {\\n  id: string;\\n  type: \\\"function\\\";\\n  function: { name: string; arguments: string };\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolDef {\\n  type: \\\"function\\\";\\n  function: {\\n    name: string;\\n    description?: string;\\n    parameters: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Adapter must implement this to be used by the tool loop. */\\nexport interface LlmAdapter {\\n  chat(messages: ChatMessage[], opts: {\\n    tools?: ToolDef[];\\n    onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n    onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  }): Promise<{ message: ChatMessage; stopReason: string }>;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Tool Loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ToolLoopOptions {\\n  maxIterations?: number;\\n  /** Context window size — enables mid-loop overflow protection. */\\n  contextWindow?: number;\\n  /** Max chars per tool result (truncated if exceeded). Default: unlimited. */\\n  maxToolResultChars?: number;\\n  /** Session ID for observation tagging. */\\n  sessionId?: string;\\n  /** Telegram chat ID for observation tagging. */\\n  chatId?: number;\\n  onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onToolStart?: (name: string, input: string) => void;\\n  onToolEnd?: (name: string, result: string, isError: boolean) => void;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS = 25;\\nconst REPETITION_THRESHOLD = 3;\\nconst RTK_ENABLED = [\\\"true\\\", \\\"1\\\"].includes(process.env.RTK_SHELL_REWRITE ?? \\\"\\\");\\n\\nexport async function runToolLoop(\\n  adapter: LlmAdapter,\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  messages: ChatMessage[],\\n  tools: ToolDef[],\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions = {},\\n): Promise<{ stopReason: string }> {\\n  const maxIter = opts.maxIterations ?? DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS;\\n  const recentCalls: string[] = []; // hashes of recent tool calls\\n  const recentToolNames: string[] = []; // rolling tool names for observation precedingTools\\n\\n  for (let i = 0; i < maxIter; i++) {\\n    // Mid-loop context overflow protection: if messages exceed 85% of context window, break early\\n    if (opts.contextWindow) {\\n      const currentTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);\\n      if (currentTokens > opts.contextWindow * 0.85) {\\n        console.warn(`[tool-loop] context overflow guard: ${currentTokens} tokens > 85% of ${opts.contextWindow} — breaking at iteration ${i}`);\\n        messages.push({\\n          role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n          content: \\\"[System: Context budget nearly exhausted. Summarize progress and respond now.]\\\",\\n        });\\n        const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n        messages.push(final.message);\\n        return { stopReason: \\\"context_overflow\\\" };\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const { message, stopReason } = await adapter.chat(messages, {\\n      tools,\\n      onText: opts.onText,\\n      onThinking: opts.onThinking,\\n    });\\n\\n    messages.push(message);\\n\\n    // Extract tool calls (OpenAI format)\\n    const toolCalls = message.tool_calls;\\n    if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {\\n      return { stopReason };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Repetition detection\\n    for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n      const hash = hashToolCall(tc.function.name, tc.function.arguments);\\n      recentCalls.push(hash);\\n    }\\n    if (detectRepetition(recentCalls)) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: \\\"[System: Tool call loop detected — same call repeated multiple times. Please summarize your progress and respond to the user.]\\\",\\n      });\\n      // One more call without tools to get a text response\\n      const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n      messages.push(final.message);\\n      return { stopReason: \\\"tool_loop_break\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Execute tool calls (parallel for different servers, serial for same)\\n    const results = await executeToolCalls(toolCalls, pool, opts, recentToolNames);\\n\\n    // Append tool results\\n    for (const r of results) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"tool\\\",\\n        tool_call_id: r.id,\\n        content: r.output,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Project auto-detect: hint agent about CodeGraph index and AI.md\\n    const usedCodegraph = toolCalls.some(tc => tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\"));\\n    const { codegraph: cgProjects, aiMd: aiProjects } = detectProjectHints(toolCalls);\\n    const hints: string[] = [];\\n\\n    if (!usedCodegraph && cgProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = cgProjects.map(p => `\\\"${p}\\\"`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`CodeGraph index detected at ${paths}. Use codegraph_context/codegraph_impact instead of grep for call-chain analysis.`);\\n      for (const p of cgProjects) hintedCodegraph.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (aiProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = aiProjects.map(p => `${p}/AI.md`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`AI.md found at ${paths} — read it for project conventions before making changes.`);\\n      for (const p of aiProjects) hintedAiMd.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (hints.length > 0) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: `[System hint: ${hints.join(\\\" | \\\")}]`,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Hit iteration limit\\n  messages.push({\\n    role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n    content: \\\"[System: Maximum tool call iterations reached. 1];\\n  let count = 0;\\n  for (let i = recent.length - 1; i >= 0 && i >= recent.length - 5; i--) {\\n    if (recent[i] === last) count++;\\n  }\\n  return count >= REPETITION_THRESHOLD;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ToolResult {\\n  id: string;\\n  output: string;\\n}\\n\\nasync function executeToolCalls(\\n  toolCalls: ToolCall[],\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions,\\n  recentToolNames: string[],\\n): Promise<ToolResult[]> {\\n  const results: ToolResult[] = [];\\n  const maxChars = opts.maxToolResultChars;\\n\\n  // Group by server to serialize calls to the same server\\n  // but parallelize across different servers\\n  const tasks = toolCalls.map(async (tc) => {\\n    const name = tc.function.name;\\n    let args: Record<string, unknown>;\\n    try {\\n      args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments);\\n    } catch {\\n      args = {};\\n    }\\n\\n    opts.onToolStart?.(name, tc.function.arguments);\\n\\n    // BC-7：在 RTK rewrite 之前快照原始 args 供 observation（否則記到 \\\"rtk git status\\\"\\n    // 而非真正的 \\\"git status\\\"）。實際 tool call 仍用改寫後的 args。\\n    const obsArgs = { ...args };\\n\\n    // RTK auto-rewrite: prefix shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output\\n    if (RTK_ENABLED && name === \\\"shell\\\" && typeof args.command === \\\"string\\\") {\\n      args.command = applyRtkPrefix(args.command);\\n    }\\n\\n    try {\\n      const result = await pool.callTool(name, args);\\n      \\n      // Codegraph enrichment: 附加 domain 資訊和 wiki 連結\\n      if (name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) {\\n        const projectPath = args.projectPath as string | undefined;\\n        enrichCodegraphResult(result, projectPath);\\n      }\\n      \\n      let output = serializeToolResult(result.content);\\n      // Truncate tool output for context-constrained models\\n      if (maxChars && output.length > maxChars) {\\n        output = output.slice(0, maxChars) + `\\\\n...[truncated, ${output.length - <observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed BC-7 regression: capture args before RTK rewrite for observations</title>
  <subtitle>Added pre-RTK snapshot of tool arguments so observations record original commands not infrastructure-rewritten versions</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added const obsArgs = { ...args } snapshot before RTK rewrite at line 230</fact>
    <fact>BC-7 comment documents rationale: record "git status" not "rtk git status" for observation corpus quality</fact>
    <fact>RTK rewrite still applied to args.command for actual tool execution (lines 233-235)</fact>
    <fact>obsArgs will be used in recordRichObservation calls instead of mutated args</fact>
    <fact>Fixes BC-7 test case in check-tool-observer.mjs which expects pre-RTK original commands</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed BC-7 observation quality regression in tool-loop.ts by capturing a snapshot of tool arguments before RTK infrastructure rewriting. The code now creates obsArgs as a shallow copy of the parsed args before the RTK rewrite mutates args.command to add the "rtk" prefix. This ensures observations.jsonl contains the user's original commands like "git status" rather than infrastructure syntax like "rtk git status", preserving corpus quality for Phase 2 analysis. The actual tool execution still uses the RTK-rewritten command for compressed output benefits. This partial fix sets up the snapshot; the next step requires updating the two obsInput object creations (success and error paths) to reference obsArgs instead of args for the observation recording.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/provider/tool-loop.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/provider/tool-loop.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Flushed by the orphan-turn-flusher.\\n   */\\n  _orphanBuffer?: string;\\n  /** Internal: debounce timer for the orphan-turn flush (armed per chunk). */\\n  _orphanFlushTimer?: NodeJS.Timeout;\\n  /**\\n   * Internal: epoch-ms until which orphan capture is suppressed. Do not ask what was done last time.\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"[End working state]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n      await unlink(wsPath);\\n      console.log(`[session] injected working-state for chat ${chatId}`);\\n    } catch { /* file doesn't exist — normal */ }\\n\\n    // Inject structured session archive restoration (complements working-state\\n    // with turn-level context, goal state, and session stats).\\n    let archiveBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    let priorArchive: SessionArchive | undefined;\\n    try {\\n      const { loadArchive, buildRestorationBlock, deleteArchive } = await import(\\\"./session-archive.js\\\");\\n      const archive = await loadArchive(chatId);\\n      if (archive) {\\n        archiveBlock = \\\"\\\\n\\\" + buildRestorationBlock(archive) + \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n        await deleteArchive(chatId);\\n        priorArchive = archive; // stash for trivial-session carry-forward on close\\n        console.log(`[session] injected session ${chatId}:`, (err as Error).message);\\n    }\\n\\n    const relayTaskBlock = await formatActiveRelayTasksForPreamble(chatId);\\n    const preambleText = breakdown.text + workingStateBlock + provider,\\n      activeProvider: activeType,\\n      sessionId: \\\"\\\",\\n      lastUsed: Date.now(),\\n      buffer: \\\"\\\",\\n      toolLog: [],\\n      stderrTail: \\\"\\\",\\n      history: [],\\n      charsIn: 0,\\n      charsOut: 0,\\n      memoryPreamble: preambleText,\\n      preambleBreakdown: {\\n        total: preambleText.length,\\n        toolNotes: breakdown.toolNotes,\\n        envBlock: breakdown.envBlock,\\n        memoryBlock: breakdown.memoryBlock,\\n        dailylogBlock: breakdown.dailylogBlock,\\n      },\\n      preambleInjected: activeType !== \\\"acp\\\" ? true : false, // Direct providers use system message; ACP needs user-message injection\\n      tailFacts: breakdown.tailFacts,\\n      injectedWikiSlugs: new Set(),\\n      injectedSkills: new Set(),\\n      inflight: false,\\n      _pendingMetaQueue: [],\\n      _consecutiveToolFails: 0,\\n      priorArchive,\\n    };\\n\\n    provider.on(\\\"update\\\", (u: SessionUpdate) => this.applyUpdate(session, u));\\n    provider.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n      // Keep the tail so we can surface it with error messages.\\n      session.stderrTail = (session.stderrTail + chunk).slice(-2000);\\n      // Surface only when debugging; keep quiet otherwise.\\n      if (process.env.DEBUG_ACP) {\\n        console.error(`[acp ${chatId}] ${chunk.trimEnd()}`);\\n      }\\n    });\\n    provider.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n      console.error(`[acp ${chatId}] agent exited (code=${code})`);\\n      // Only fire the unexpected-exit hook if this session is still registered.\\n      // Planned shutdowns (drop/sweepIdle/shutdown) remove it from the map first.\\n      const stillRegistered = this.sessions.get(chatId) === session;\\n      if (stillRegistered) {\\n        this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n        if (this.onUnexpectedExit) {\\n          this.onUnexpectedExit(session, code).catch((e) =>\\n            console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onUnexpectedExit failed:`, e),\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n    });\\n\\n    session.sessionId = await provider.initialize();\\n    this.sessions.set(chatId, session);\\n    logEvent(\\\"session_start\\\", { ProviderType, env: Record<string, string>, systemPrompt?: string): Provider {\\n    if (type === \\\"acp\\\") {\\n      return new AcpProvider({\\n        command: config.agent.command,\\n        args: config.agent.args,\\n        cwd: config.sessionCwd,\\n        env,\\n        promptRetries: config.acpPromptRetries,\\n        promptRetryBaseMs: config.acpPromptRetryBaseMs,\\n have limited context — keep the server set focused.\\n      mcpServerFilter: type === \\\"local\\\" ? [\\\"memory\\\", \\\"notebooklm\\\", \\\"google\\\"] : undefined,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Switch the active provider for a chat. Closes the old session and\\n   * creates a new one with the specified provider type.\\n   * Returns the new session.\\n   */\\n  async switchProvider(chatId: number, type: ProviderType, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing) {\\n      this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n      await existing.client.close().catch(() => {});\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId, type);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Start (or restart) the inactivity timer for a turn. */\\n  startInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    this.clearInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const ms = session._toolInFlight && config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs > 0\\n      ? config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs\\n      : config.turnInactivityTimeoutMs;\\n    if (ms <= 0) return;\\n    session._inactivityTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[session ${session.chatId}] turn inactivity timeout (${ms}ms, toolInFlight=${!!session._toolInFlight}) — auto-cancelling`,\\n      );\\n      session.client.cancel();\\n    }, ms);\\n    session._inactivityTimer.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  private resetInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (!session._inactivityTimer) return;\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  clearInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (session._inactivityTimer) {\\n      clearTimeout(session._inactivityTimer);\\n      session._inactivityTimer = undefined;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private applyUpdate(session: ChatSession, u: SessionUpdate) {\\n    this.resetInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    // Opportunistically capture usage-like meta the agent may attach.\\n    const meta = (up as { _meta?: Record<string, unknown> })._meta;\\n    const usage =\\n      (meta?.usage as Record<string, unknown> | undefined) ??\\n      ((up as { usage?: Record<string, unknown> }).usage);\\n    if (usage && typeof usage === \\\"object\\\") {\\n      session.usage = usage;\\n    }\\n    switch (up.sessionUpdate) {\\n      case \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\":\\n      case \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\": {\\n        const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n        if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n          // Orphan turn: a message chunk arrived with no prompt turn in-flight\\n          // (typically a background sub-agent completion re-invoking the agent\\n          // via task-notification). The normal request→response flush path in\\n          // runPrompt isn't listening, so route it to the debounced orphan\\n          // flusher instead of the live-turn buffer. Thought chunks are never\\n          // user-facing, so only real message chunks take this path.\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" && !session.inflight) {\\n            this.appendOrphan(session, content.text);\\n            break;\\n          }\\n          const wasEmpty = session.buffer.length === 0 && !session.thinkingBuffer;\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") {\\n            session.thinkingBuffer = (session.thinkingBuffer || \\\"\\\") + content.text;\\n          } else {\\n            session.buffer += content.text;\\n            session.charsOut += content.text.length;\\n          }\\n          if (wasEmpty) petState(\\\"streaming\\\");\\n          // Early-capture RELAY_DELEGATE tokens during streaming.\\n          // Workaround: Kiro CLI may strip <<...>> from final text in pure-text turns,\\n          // but streaming chunks arrive unfiltered. []);\\n          session._streamingDelegates = captured.length ? captured : undefined;\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call\\\": {\\n        const title = (up.title as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\";\\n        session.toolLog.push(`🔧 ${title}`);\\n        session._toolInFlight = true;\\n        petState(\\\"tool\\\", title);\\n        session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call_update\\\": {\\n        const status = up.status as string | undefined;\\n        if (status === \\\"completed\\\") {\\n          session.toolLog.push(`✅ ${(up.toolCallId as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\"}`);\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails = 0;\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        } else if (status === \\\"failed\\\") {\\n          session.toolLog.push(`❌ ${(up.toolCallId as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\"}`);\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails++;\\n          if (session._consecutiveToolFails >= 2) {\\n            session.toolLog.push(\\n              `⚠️ [Reflexion hint] ${session._consecutiveToolFails} consecutive tool failures. ` +\\n              `STOP and diagnose: what assumption is wrong? Try a different approach.`\\n            );\\n          }\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      default:\\n        // Ignore plan/user_message_chunk/etc. for the bridge.\\n        break;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Buffer an orphan-turn message chunk and (re)arm the quiescence timer.\\n   * Each new chunk pushes the flush out by orphanFlushQuiescenceMs so a\\n   * multi-chunk background summary is delivered as one message once the\\n   * agent goes quiet. No-op if the flusher is disabled (quiescence 0).\\n   */\\n  private appendOrphan(session: ChatSession, text: string) {\\n    if (config.orphanFlushQuiescenceMs <= 0) return;\\n    if (session._orphanSuppressUntil && Date.now() < session._orphanSuppressUntil) return;\\n    session._orphanBuffer = (session._orphanBuffer || \\\"\\\") + text;\\n    session.charsOut += text.length;\\n    if (session._orphanFlushTimer) clearTimeout(session._orphanFlushTimer);\\n    session._orphanFlushTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n      session._orphanFlushTimer = undefined;\\n      this.flushOrphan(session);\\n    }, config.orphanFlushQuiescenceMs);\\n    session._orphanFlushTimer.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Flush any buffered orphan-turn text immediately. Fired by the quiescence\\n   * timer, and eagerly at the start of a new live turn (resetTurn) so orphan\\n   * output never interleaves with the live turn's streaming placeholder.\\n   * Delivery itself is delegated to `onOrphanTurn` (run-prompt flusher).\\n   */\\n  flushOrphan(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (session._orphanFlushTimer) {\\n      clearTimeout(session._orphanFlushTimer);\\n      session._orphanFlushTimer = undefined;\\n    }\\n    const text = (session._orphanBuffer || \\\"\\\").trim();\\n    session._orphanBuffer = undefined;\\n    if (!text) return;\\n    if (!this.onOrphanTurn) {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[orphan ${session.chatId}] buffered ${text.length} chars but no onOrphanTurn handler wired — dropping`,\\n      );\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${session.chatId}] flushing orphan turn (${text.length} chars)`);\\n    logEvent(\\\"orphan_flush\\\", { chars: text.length }, { chatId: session.chatId, userId: session.userId <observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Completed BC-7 fix: both observation paths now use pre-RTK args snapshot</title>
  <subtitle>Updated success and error obsInput objects to reference obsArgs, ensuring observations record original commands</subtitle>
  <facts>

    <fact>Updated error path obsInput at line 277 to use args: obsArgs instead of args: args</fact>
    <fact>Both recordRichObservation calls now receive pre-RTK command arguments</fact>
    <fact>Tool execution still uses RTK-rewritten args for actual tool calls, only observation records snapshot</fact>
    <fact>Fixes BC-7 test case expectation that observations contain "git status" not "rtk git status"</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Completed the BC-7 regression fix by updating both observation recording sites to use the pre-RTK args snapshot. The success path (completed tools) and error path (failed tools) both now pass obsArgs to their respective obsInput objects before calling recordRichObservation. This ensures observations.jsonl records the user's original commands like "git status" rather than infrastructure-rewritten versions like "rtk git status", preserving corpus quality for Phase 2 analysis. The actual tool execution continues to benefit from RTK compression by using the rewritten args. This fix addresses the DirectProvider observation quality issue. The remaining major work is restoring seq observation infrastructure in sessionManager.ts for the ACP adapter path, which is completely missing recording logic.</narrative>

// Surface only when debugging; keep quiet otherwise.\\n      if (process.env.DEBUG_ACP) {\\n        console.error(`[acp ${chatId}] ${chunk.trimEnd()}`);\\n      }\\n    });\\n    provider.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n      console.error(`[acp ${chatId}] agent exited (code=${code})`);\\n      // Only fire the unexpected-exit hook if this session is still registered.\\n failed:`, e),\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n    });\\n\\n    session.sessionId = await provider.initialize();\\n    this.sessions.set(chatId, session);\\n    logEvent(\\\"session_start\\\", { provider: activeType, sessionId: session.sessionId }, { chatId, userId });\\n    return session;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Factory: create the right Provider based on type. */\\n  private createProvider(type: ProviderType, env: Record<string, string>, systemPrompt?: string): Provider {\\n    if (type === \\\"acp\\\") {\\n      return new AcpProvider({\\n        command: config.agent.command,\\n No-op if the flusher is disabled (quiescence 0).\\n   */\\n  private appendOrphan(session: ChatSession, text: string) {\\n    if (config.orphanFlushQuiescenceMs <= 0) return;\\n    if (session._orphanSuppressUntil && Date.now() < session._orphanSuppressUntil) return;\\n    session._orphanBuffer = (session._orphanBuffer || \\\"\\\") + text;\\n    session.charsOut += text.length;\\n    if (session._orphanFlushTimer) clearTimeout(session._orphanFlushTimer);\\n    session._orphanFlushTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n      session._orphanFlushTimer = undefined;\\n      this.flushOrphan(session);\\n    }, config.orphanFlushQuiescenceMs);\\n    session._orphanFlushTimer.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Flush any buffered orphan-turn text immediately. itself is delegated to `onOrphanTurn` (run-prompt flusher).\\n   */\\n  flushOrphan(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (session._orphanFlushTimer) {\\n      clearTimeout(session._orphanFlushTimer);\\n (!this.onOrphanTurn) {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[orphan ${session.chatId}] buffered ${text.length} chars but no onOrphanTurn handler wired — dropping`,\\n      );\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${session.chatId}] flushing orphan turn (${text.length} chars)`);\\n    logEvent(\\\"orphan_flush\\\", { chars: text.length }, { chatId: session.chatId, userId: session.userId });\\n    Promise.resolve(this.onOrphanTurn(session, text)).catch((err) =>\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${session.chatId}] onOrphanTurn failed:`, err),\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  resetTurn(session: ChatSession) {\\n    // Flush any pending orphan output before a new live turn clears the\\n    // buffers, so background continuation isn't lost or interleaved with the\\n    // upcoming turn's streaming placeholder.\\n    if (session._orphanBuffer || session._orphanFlushTimer) this.flushOrphan(session);\\n    session._orphanSuppressUntil = undefined;\\n    session.buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n n + h.text.length : n),\\n      0,\\n    );\\n    const substantive = s.history.length >= config.archiveMinTurns && assistantChars > 0;\\n    try {\\n      if (substantive) {\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      } else if (s.priorArchive) {\\n        // Trivial close — keep the prior, more-substantive archive.\\n        await saveArchive(s.priorArchive);\\n        console.log(`[archive] chat ${s.chatId}: trivial close (${s.history.length} turns), carried prior archive forward`);\\n      } else if (s.history.length > 0) {\\n        // No prior to fall back to — persist what little we have.\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      }\\n    } catch (e) {\\n      console.error(`[session ${s.chatId}] archive failed:`, e);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  async drop(chatId: number, closeReason: \\\"manual\\\" | \\\"reset\\\" | \\\"restart\\\" = \\\"manual\\\") {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n    // Archive session state before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n    if (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive) {\\n      await this.archiveOnClose(s, closeReason);\\n    }\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n        console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    await s.client.close();\\n  }\\n\\n  private async sweepIdle() {\\n    const now = Date.now();\\n    for (const [chatId, s] of this.sessions) {\\n      // --- Runtime Health Monitor ---\\n      // Detect subprocess that silently died (pid no longer valid) but\\n      // exit event hasn't fired yet (possible on Windows when parent shell\\n      // exits but Node doesn't see the signal).\\n      const clientPid = \\\"pid\\\" in s.client ? } catch (e) {\\n            console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n          }\\n        }\\n        s.client.close().catch(() => {});\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  async shutdown() {\\n    clearInterval(this.sweeper);\\n    const all = Array.from(this.sessions.values());\\n    this.sessions.clear();\\n    console.log(`[session] shutdown: flushing ${all.length} session(s)`);\\n    // Archive all sessions before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n    for (const s of all) {\\n      if (!s.client.isClosed && (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive)) {\\n        await this.archiveOnClose(s, \\\"restart\\\");\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      for (const s of all) {\\n        if (s.client.isClosed) {\\n          console.log(`[session] shutdown: skipping already-closed chat ${s.chatId}`);\\n          continue;\\n        }\\n        console.log(`[session] shutdown: running onBeforeClose for chat ${s.chatId} (history=${s.history.length})`);\\n        try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); <observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Added seq observation infrastructure fields to ChatSession interface</title>
  <subtitle>Restored _toolTitles Map and _recentToolNames array to ChatSession for ACP path observation recording</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added _toolTitles optional Map field mapping opaque toolCallIds to human-readable tool names</fact>
    <fact>Added _recentToolNames optional array field for rolling tool name sequence with turn boundary markers</fact>
    <fact>_toolTitles comment explains: tool_call binds title to toolCallId, tool_call_update resolves name, delete after use</fact>
    <fact>_recentToolNames comment explains: rolling sequence for Tier A seq observation precedingTools, maintained only in tool_call case, resetTurn pushes TURN_MARKER without clearing</fact>
    <fact>Both fields placed after _consecutiveToolFails to maintain interface structure</fact>
    <fact>Reading tool_call and tool_call_update handlers (lines 434-469) to prepare for wiring seq recording logic</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Added the two critical infrastructure fields to ChatSession interface for seq observation recording. The _toolTitles Map resolves opaque toolCallIds to human-readable names since tool_call_update events only carry the id, not the name - the name is captured at tool_call start and looked up at completion/failure. The _recentToolNames array maintains the rolling sequence of tool names with turn boundary markers for precedingTools in seq observations. The design explicitly maintains this array across turns (resetTurn pushes TURN_MARKER but doesn't clear) so each tool's precedingTools reflects realistic temporal context while giving Phase 2 analyzer clear turn boundaries. The session is now reading the tool_call and tool_call_update handlers to implement the actual recording logic: capturing title in tool_call, resolving name and recording seq observations in tool_call_update's completed/failed branches.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

n + h.text.length : n),\\n      0,\\n    );\\n    const substantive = s.history.length >= config.archiveMinTurns && assistantChars > 0;\\n    try {\\n      if (substantive) {\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      } else if (s.priorArchive) {\\n        // Trivial close — keep the prior, more-substantive archive.\\n        await saveArchive(s.priorArchive);\\n        console.log(`[archive] chat ${s.chatId}: trivial close (${s.history.length} turns), carried prior archive forward`);\\n      } else if (s.history.length > 0) {\\n        // No prior to fall back to — persist what little we have.\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      }\\n    } catch (e) {\\n      console.error(`[session ${s.chatId}] archive failed:`, e);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  async drop(chatId: number, closeReason: \\\"manual\\\" | \\\"reset\\\" | \\\"restart\\\" = \\\"manual\\\") {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n    // Archive session state before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n    if (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive) {\\n      await this.archiveOnClose(s, closeReason);\\n    }\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n        console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    await s.client.close();\\n  }\\n\\n  private async sweepIdle() {\\n    const now = Date.now();\\n    for (const [chatId, s] of this.sessions) {\\n      // --- Runtime Health Monitor ---\\n      // Detect subprocess that silently died (pid no longer valid) but\\n      // exit event hasn't fired yet (possible on Windows when parent shell\\n      // exits but Node doesn't see the signal).\\n      const clientPid = \\\"pid\\\" in s.client ? // skip idle check for this session\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      // --- Idle timeout sweep ---\\n      if (now - s.lastUsed > config.sessionIdleMs) {\\n        console.log(`[session] dropping idle chat ${chatId}`);\\n        this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n        // Archive session state before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n        if (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive) {\\n          await this.archiveOnClose(s, \\\"idle\\\");\\n        }\\n        if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n          try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); content.text:
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Wired tool_call case to maintain seq observation infrastructure</title>
  <subtitle>Added toolCallId-to-title mapping and rolling tool name tracking for precedingTools in ACP path</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Extracts toolCallId from update and stores title binding in _toolTitles Map for later resolution</fact>
    <fact>Calls appendToolName to push tool title into _recentToolNames rolling array</fact>
    <fact>Comment documents this as "Tier A precedingTools 唯一 push 維護點" - single maintenance point to avoid double-counting</fact>
    <fact>Uses nullish coalescing ??= to lazily initialize Map and array on first use</fact>
    <fact>tool_call fires before tool_call_update, so title is captured early and tool name is appended before completion/failure</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Wired the tool_call case to maintain seq observation infrastructure. When an ACP tool starts, the code now captures the opaque toolCallId and binds it to the human-readable title in _toolTitles Map, enabling later resolution in tool_call_update which only receives the id. The title is also pushed into _recentToolNames via appendToolName for precedingTools tracking. The comment emphasizes this is the single maintenance point to avoid double-counting - tool_call and tool_call_update both fire per tool, but only tool_call should update the sequence. Lazy initialization with ??= ensures Maps and arrays are created on demand. This prepares the infrastructure for the next step: modifying tool_call_update's completed/failed branches to resolve toolCallId to toolName and call recordSeqObservation with precedingTools from _recentToolNames.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

1. Restored tool-observer.ts from pre-merge version with dual-tier design
2. Fixed BC-7 in tool-loop.ts (pre-RTK args snapshot)
3. Added tool-observer imports to sessionManager.ts
4. Added _toolTitles and _recentToolNames fields to ChatSession
5. Wired tool_call case to capture toolCallId→title mapping and push to _recentToolNames
6. Wired tool_call_update to:
   - Resolve toolCallId to toolName via _toolTitles
   - Record seq observations for completed/failed tools (ACP only)
   - Use slice(0,-1) to exclude current tool from precedingTools
   - Delete toolCallId from _toolTitles after use
   - Add logEvent calls with resolved toolName

Now they're looking at resetTurn to add the TURN_MARKER logic. Let me observe what happens next.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Fully restored seq observation recording in ACP tool_call_update handler</title>
  <subtitle>Added toolCallId resolution, logEvent calls, and recordSeqObservation for completed/failed tools in ACP path</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added toolCallId resolution logic: _toolTitles.get(updId) fallback to inline title fallback to "tool"</fact>
    <fact>Both completed and failed branches now call recordSeqObservation when activeProvider === "acp"</fact>
    <fact>precedingTools uses slice(0, -1) to exclude current tool (R-2 fix) since tool_call already pushed it</fact>
    <fact>Observations include name, status, sessionId, chatId, and precedingTools from _recentToolNames</fact>
    <fact>Added logEvent calls with resolved toolName instead of raw toolCallId for readable event logs</fact>
    <fact>toolCallId deleted from _toolTitles Map after use to prevent memory leaks in long sessions</fact>
    <fact>BC-1 behavior contract enforced: seq only for ACP, Direct uses rich tier in tool-loop</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Completed the core seq observation restoration by wiring tool_call_update to record observations for ACP adapter paths. The code resolves opaque toolCallIds to human-readable names via the _toolTitles Map populated in tool_call, with fallbacks for robustness. Both completed and failed tool branches now call recordSeqObservation when the session's activeProvider is "acp", passing name, status, sessionId, chatId, and precedingTools. The R-2 regression fix (slice(0, -1)) ensures precedingTools excludes the current tool since it was already pushed in tool_call, aligning with rich tier semantics. The logEvent calls now use resolved toolName instead of displaying raw opaque toolCallIds in logs. Map entries are cleaned up after use to prevent memory leaks. BC-1 guard ensures Direct provider doesn't double-record - it writes rich observations in tool-loop. Next step: add TURN_MARKER logic to resetTurn around line 552 to delimit turn boundaries without clearing _recentToolNames.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Completed seq observation restoration with TURN_MARKER logic in resetTurn</title>
  <subtitle>Added turn boundary marker insertion and toolTitles cleanup to resetTurn, preserving cross-turn context for precedingTools</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>resetTurn now clears _toolTitles Map to prevent memory leaks from per-turn toolCallId-to-name bindings</fact>
    <fact>Pushes TURN_MARKER to _recentToolNames when array has content, WITHOUT clearing the array</fact>
    <fact>Comment explains design rationale: clearing array would make first tool per turn always have empty precedingTools</fact>
    <fact>TURN_MARKER lets Phase 2 analyzer detect turn boundaries and cut tool combinations across turns</fact>
    <fact>Design maintains realistic temporal context (tools from recent turns) while preventing cross-turn false patterns</fact>
    <fact>Completes the seq observation restoration: tool-observer.ts restored, tool-loop BC-7 fixed, sessionManager fully wired</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Completed the seq observation tier restoration by adding TURN_MARKER logic to resetTurn. The method now clears the per-turn _toolTitles Map to prevent memory leaks in long-running sessions, and conditionally pushes TURN_MARKER to _recentToolNames when the array has content. Critically, the array is NOT cleared - clearing would make every turn's first tool have empty precedingTools, losing valuable temporal context. Instead, TURN_MARKER acts as a delimiter that Phase 2 analyzer can use to cut tool combinations at turn boundaries, preventing false pattern detection across user interactions while maintaining realistic sequence history. This sophisticated design lets each tool's precedingTools reflect recent cross-turn context (valuable for understanding workflows) while giving analyzers clear signals to avoid spurious multi-turn combinations. The entire dual-tier observation system is now restored: tool-observer.ts has both seq and rich tiers, tool-loop captures pre-RTK args for rich observations, and sessionManager records seq observations for ACP paths with proper turn delimitation.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_modified>
</observation>
